question: Q4: Tools & Packages

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve used RevenueCat.
sections.hint: A service that manages subscriptions, receipts, and cross-platform billing
no_hands: Why haven’t you tried it yet — too costly, too complex, or not needed?
If you were to use it tomorrow, which part of your app would you connect first?
hands_up: What problem did RevenueCat solve for you?
What turned out easier than expected?
Did you hit any missing features or limits?
follow_ups: Did anyone here stop using RevenueCat? Why?
Who has switched to something else?
What’s one feature you wish RevenueCat offered?

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve used the in_app_purchase package.
sections.hint: Flutter’s official package for App Store and Play Store billing
no_hands: Why not use it — do you prefer third-party tools, or is monetization not relevant for your app?
What would make you try it in the future?
hands_up: What went smoothly when you used it?
What was the most difficult part?
How did you test billing across iOS and Android?
What did you learn about handling failed payments or refunds?
follow_ups: Who switched away from in_app_purchase? Why?
Who prefers it to third-party services?
What’s the strongest reason to stick with it?

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve integrated AdMob or another ads SDK.
sections.hint: Google’s SDK for showing ads inside apps
no_hands: Why don’t ad SDKs feel like a good fit for your apps?
What concerns do you have — user experience, revenue, or complexity?
If you had to test ads, which format would you start with?
hands_up: Which ad formats worked best for you?
How did you balance ads with user experience?
What setup step was the most painful?
What’s one thing you wish you knew before adding ads?
follow_ups: Who swapped AdMob for another network?
Who found ad revenue stronger than expected?
Who has a tip for making ads less annoying?

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve built your own custom backend for monetization.
sections.hint: A server that manages receipts, fraud checks, or subscription status
no_hands: Why haven’t you built one — too much work, or not needed?
If you had to build one, what would be the first feature you’d include?
hands_up: Why did you decide to build your own backend?
What was the hardest technical part?
How do you handle fraud, cancellations, and renewals?
follow_ups: Who switched from a custom backend to a service like RevenueCat?
Who thinks building your own backend is the only way to stay in control?
What’s one mistake to avoid when building your own?

wrap_up: Which tool or package would you recommend to someone new to Flutter monetization?
Which one caused you the most pain — and why?
Do you prefer 'use one package and ship fast' or 'build your own backend for full control'?
What’s one improvement you’d like to see in Flutter’s monetization tools?
